---
title: "Output Files"
---

# Output files

All outputs are written to the **`results/`** folder by default (configurable via `--output-dir`).

## Naming convention

For an input file `genes.txt`, outputs use the prefix `results/genes`:

| File | Required option |
|------|-----------------|
| `genes_annotation.html` | always (except `--csv-only`) |
| `genes_annotation.csv` | always (except `--html-only`) |
| `genes_annotation.xlsx` | `--excel` |
| `genes_annotation.json` | `--json` |
| `genes_summary.txt` | always |
| `genes_manifest.json` | always |

For a single gene (`--gene RAD51,homo_sapiens`):

```text
results/RAD51_homo_sapiens_annotation.html
results/RAD51_homo_sapiens_summary.txt
...
```

## Format descriptions

### HTML (`*_annotation.html`)

- Interactive table with **DataTables** (sort, search, pagination);
- Color-coded cells by source (NCBI, Ensembl, UniProt, etc.);
- Clickable links to databases — each ID opens in a **new browser tab**;
- Coverage report at the bottom of the page.

Open directly in a browser or via the Flask viewer.
When viewed inside the GeneWeave web viewer, external sites are never loaded in the iframe (they block framing); the link opens a new tab instead.
### CSV (`*_annotation.csv`)

- **Semicolon** (`;`) separator — Excel-friendly in many locales;
- Plain text (HTML links converted to text);
- UTF-8 encoding.

### Excel (`*_annotation.xlsx`)

- "Annotations" sheet with formatted headers;
- Auto-sized columns;
- Requires `openpyxl`.

### JSON (`*_annotation.json`)

Structure:

```json
{
  "columns": ["Gene Symbol and Organism", "..."],
  "rows": [["RAD51,homo_sapiens", "..."], "..."],
  "summary": {
    "total_genes": 12,
    "coverage_percent": 72.3,
    "errors": []
  },
  "metadata": {
    "version": "1.1.0",
    "generated_at": "2026-07-06T21:48:01Z"
  }
}
```

Suitable for integration into bioinformatics pipelines.

### Text summary (`*_summary.txt`)

Human-readable report listing:

- Genes analyzed and complete genes;
- Overall coverage (%);
- Per-column statistics;
- Failed genes (if any).

### Manifest (`*_manifest.json`)

Run metadata:

```json
{
  "version": "1.1.0",
  "generated_at": "2026-07-06T21:48:01Z",
  "duration_seconds": 45.2,
  "total_genes": 12,
  "coverage_percent": 72.3,
  "errors": [],
  "outputs": {
    "html": "results/genes_annotation.html",
    "csv": "results/genes_annotation.csv"
  }
}
```

Useful for tracking runs and automating workflows.

## Local cache

| File | Location | Description |
|------|----------|-------------|
| `gene_annotations.json` | `.cache/` | One entry per annotated gene (raw table data) |

Useful commands:

```bash
python3 main.py --cache-info
python3 main.py genes.txt --clear-cache
python3 main.py genes.txt --no-cache
```

## Interpreting "No data found"

A `No data found` cell means **the queried database returned no result** for that gene and organism. Common causes:

- Poorly annotated organism in Ensembl or UniProt;
- Ambiguous or obsolete gene symbol;
- No PDB structure or KEGG pathway for that gene;
- Temporary API limit (retry later).

This is **not a pipeline error** — annotation of other columns continues normally.

## Project folders

```text
projet NGS/
├── annotation/              # Core Python package
│   ├── cli.py               # CLI orchestration
│   ├── gui.py               # Tkinter GUI
│   ├── config.py            # Configuration
│   ├── databases/           # API clients
│   └── templates/           # HTML table templates
├── viewer/                  # Flask web viewer
├── data/                    # Sample input files
├── docs/                    # Quarto documentation
├── results/                 # Default output directory
└── main.py                  # CLI entry point
```
